content_of_post_to_comment: You don’t get over a parent treating you with cruelty. You simply don’t. You can heal and you grow and you become free and happy. But there is a deep wounding in being treated with cruelty by someone who was meant to love you tenderly. You deserve softeness, not to be told to ‘get over it.’
comment: The 'get over it' framing assumes harm is a completed event you can file away, but with parental cruelty the past keeps updating present trust calculations; healing doesn't end its relevance.
